summary: REVIEW-001 consolidation treated the architecture as broadly viable but exposed localized maintainability and boundary issues that needed guided development.
notes: Transition from the concern for the macro level (hexagonal architecture) to the concern for structrue inside the layers.

summary: REVIEW-001 domain/core findings indicated that core architecture was workable but needed clearer boundaries and ownership. (review-domain-core.adoc, lines 342–357.)
notes: Early signal before the later domain/meta vocabulary became explicit.

summary: REVIEW-001 adapter/CLI pass contributed the first guided architecture-development concern around repository boundaries.
notes: Kept as context, not as current closure evidence.

summary: REVIEW-002 final summary showed a positive architecture trend and shifted attention toward semantic friction and repayable localized debt.
notes: Supports the current slice-based semantic-clarification posture.

summary: REVIEW-002 hotspot analysis reframed maintainability as selective hotspot work rather than broad cleanup.
notes: Reason for keeping the concern watched but not turning it into a mechanical cleanup wave.

summary: REVIEW-002 adapter boundary pass found the boundary structurally sound, with residual seam-hardening and vocabulary issues to watch.
notes: This concern is not closed even though the trend was positive.

summary: REVIEW-003 public-surface pass made tutorials, user guide, demo path, and onboarding confidence visible as steering concerns.
notes: Not just cosmetic documentation polish.

summary: REVIEW-003 release-readiness review showed that public-surface and release mechanics were real readiness concerns.
notes: The concern remains watched after the 0.2 release path rather than closed.

summary: REVIEW-003 release-readiness context raised adoption confidence, which depends partly on clear workbook projection and editing surfaces.
notes: Indirect signal; concrete projection work remains tracked elsewhere.

summary: Workbook option vocabulary is triplicated and divergent across write and read paths.
notes: Selected current finding for adapter/spreadsheet-boundary watch state.

summary: Module-size and modularization debt remains the current tracker for selective package-surface work.
notes: Heatmap signal, not instruction to split every large file.

summary: Column roles remain an interim contract needing a decision before further cleanup.
notes: Current domain/meta semantic pressure.

summary: workbook_views deferred-execution language needs cross-layer audit with rendering.
notes: Projection concern should be revisited with workbook-view contract work.

summary: compact_multiaxis package formation remains deferred until dense_axes or concrete pressure appears.
notes: Projection pressure exists, but not enough to justify a package split by itself.

summary: Package-internal examples became less discoverable from a repository browse after root examples cleanup.
notes: Selected adoption-surface signal; not a broad examples cleanup directive.

summary: ODS parity currently has XLSX as its only comparison backend.
notes: Supports watch posture without creating immediate portability work.

summary: Concern threads are introduced as a steering layer above reviews and findings while preserving flat canonical relation tables.
notes: First implementation slice; not a historical reconstruction.

summary: REVIEW-001 consolidation treated the architecture as broadly viable but exposed localized maintainability and boundary issues that needed guided development.
notes: Transition from the concern for the macro level (hexagonal architecture) to the concern for structure inside the layers.

summary: REVIEW-001 findings were converted into a guided backlog/FTR response instead of being treated as loose review comments.
notes: Coarse activity signal: establishes the review-driven architecture workflow after REVIEW-001.

summary: Critical REVIEW-001 risks were closed through quick wins, JSON workbook-meta serialization, and moving backend-neutral formula intent out of rendering.
notes: Coarse activity signal for the immediate architecture-risk closure wave; commit refs are anchors, not full provenance.

summary: Rendering pass structure, backend dispatch, pipeline registry, and runner responsibilities were consolidated into clearer ownership seams.
notes: Coarse activity signal: architecture cleanup happened in bounded slices rather than a broad rewrite.

summary: P4A projection, resource, artifact-manifest, workbook-view, reimport, and ODS parity slices exercised the architecture against richer roundtrip scenarios.
notes: Coarse activity signal: shows feature pressure being used as architecture proof rather than isolated feature work.

summary: FK-helper and discriminator package splits proved that large modules could be decomposed with re-export and boundary guards.
notes: Coarse activity signal immediately before REVIEW-002; supports the later conclusion that refactoring should remain selective and guard-backed.
